title: Melatonin synthesis
script_summary: A brief exploration of how melatonin is synthesized in our bodies and its importance.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about melatonin synthesis than social media does.
Melatonin is the hormone that regulates sleep, but how does our body make it?
It all starts in the pineal gland, a tiny organ in your brain that senses light.
As darkness falls, the gland converts serotonin into melatonin through a series of steps.
This process tells your body it's time to rest, helping you sleep better and feel refreshed.
Curious about more science secrets? Hit that follow for more!
